1) Ensure Cargo.toml metadata: license, readme, repository, keywords/categories, publish=true.
2) Tag/version alignment: tag vX.Y.Z must match package.version.
3) CI: publish job gated on tags; requires CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN; skip prerelease tags.
4) Order: publish dependency crates first (asupersync/rich_rust/charmed_rust), then pi_agent_rust.
5) Validate with cargo package + cargo publish --dry-run.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3u1y","title":"Gap: nicobailon-interview-tool missing embedded asset (form/index.html)","description":"# Problem
community/nicobailon-interview-tool fails because it references a form/index.html file that is not present in the extension directory. This is an artifact completeness issue.

# Root cause
The extension expects an embedded HTML file (form/index.html) to be bundled alongside it. Either:
1. The file was not downloaded/extracted when the corpus was assembled
2. The extension has a build step that generates it

# Action
- Check the extension source repo for the file
- If it exists upstream, add it to the corpus
- If it requires a build step, document as a known limitation

# Evidence
- `tests/ext_conformance/reports/random_trials/RANDOM_TRIALS_REPORT.md`
- `target/ext_conformance/logs/random_trials/trial_seed_42.jsonl`","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"MistyCliff","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:51:39.162776792Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T01:44:13.481106386Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T01:44:13.481002302Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":636,"issue_id":"bd-3u1y","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resetting to open — stalled with no progress from previous assignee. Available for pickup.","created_at":"2026-02-07T00:57:29Z"},{"id":648,"issue_id":"bd-3u1y","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ScarletGate: Investigated. Extension server.ts (line 227-232) reads 3 embedded files at module load time via readFileSync:
- form/index.html
- form/styles.css  
- form/script.js
- form/themes/ directory

These are build-time or repo-included assets not captured in the corpus snapshot. The extension fundamentally can't load without them. Options:
1. Download from upstream repo (manual, fragile)
2. Accept as permanent N/A (embedded asset dependency)

Recommendation: Accept as N/A. This is an artifact completeness issue, not a resolver/shim gap. Closing as won't-fix.","created_at":"2026-02-07T01:44:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3u81","title":"Preflight: include CARGO_TARGET_DIR and CARGO_HOME in disk checks","description":"scripts/e2e/run_all.sh preflight currently checks repo/artifacts/TMPDIR headroom but does not explicitly probe CARGO_TARGET_DIR and CARGO_HOME paths. This can miss ENOSPC risk when cargo caches/target are redirected to different mounts. Extend check_disk_headroom to probe CARGO_TARGET_DIR (or PROJECT_ROOT/target fallback) and CARGO_HOME (or HOME/.cargo fallback), using tmp/cargo threshold budget and clear target labels in output.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-08T07:57:13.093605306Z","created_by":"root","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:00:16.895401565Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T08:00:16.895378803Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["infra","quality","tests"],"comments":[{"id":830,"issue_id":"bd-3u81","author":"root","text":"Implemented in scripts/e2e/run_all.sh: preflight now probes CARGO_TARGET_DIR and CARGO_HOME (in addition to repo/artifacts/TMPDIR), applies tmp/cargo threshold (VERIFY_MIN_TMP_FREE_MB), and updates guidance docs/help text. Validation: bash -n scripts/e2e/run_all.sh; ./scripts/e2e/run_all.sh --profile quick --skip-lint --skip-unit exits 2 in low-space environment and now reports cargo_target/cargo_home targets explicitly.","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:00:10Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3v0j.6","title":"Collapsible tool output blocks in conversation viewport","description":"# Goal
Add collapse/expand toggles to tool output blocks in the conversation viewport, so long tool results (grep with 200 matches, find with 1000 files) dont push important conversation content off-screen.

# Background
When a tool returns a large result (e.g., grep with 200 matches), the entire output is rendered inline in the conversation viewport. This pushes the AIs analysis and the users next input far off-screen, requiring extensive scrolling. In Claude Code and similar tools, tool outputs are collapsible — showing a summary line with an expand toggle.

# Implementation Plan

1. Tool output block structure:
   ▼ bash: cargo test (exit 0, 847 lines, 12.3s)    [collapse]
     running 90 tests
     test sse::tests::parse_basic ... ok
     test sse::tests::parse_multi ... ok
     ... (847 lines total)

   Collapsed:
   ▶ bash: cargo test (exit 0, 847 lines, 12.3s)    [expand]

2. Auto-collapse rules:
   - Tool output > 20 lines: auto-collapsed, show first 5 lines as preview
   - Tool output <= 20 lines: expanded by default
   - Configurable threshold in settings.json
   - User can always toggle manually

3. Interaction:
   - Click (or Enter when block focused) toggles collapse/expand
   - Keybinding: Tab cycles through collapsible blocks
   - Visual indicator: ▶ (collapsed) / ▼ (expanded) prefix
   - Summary line always visible: tool name, exit code, line count, duration

4. Implementation in viewport:
   - ViewportContent entries get a CollapsibleBlock variant
   - CollapsibleBlock { summary: String, content: String, is_collapsed: bool }
   - Viewport height calculation accounts for collapsed blocks (1 line vs full height)
   - Toggle updates viewport scroll position to keep context stable

5. Persistence:
   - Collapse state is session-transient (reset on session reload)
   - Not persisted to JSONL

# Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs: CollapsibleBlock in viewport, toggle logic, keybinding
- May need viewport content model changes

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Large tool outputs auto-collapsed with preview
- [ ] Small tool outputs expanded by default
- [ ] Toggle works via keyboard
- [ ] Summary line shows tool name, exit code, duration, line count
- [ ] Viewport scroll position stable on toggle
- [ ] Configurable auto-collapse threshold
- [ ] Works with all 7 tool types","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:59:57.239953877Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:02:13.213938732Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T08:02:13.213824659Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tui","ux","viewport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:59:57.239953877Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":518,"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN REFINEMENT (from review):

Collapsible blocks should apply to ALL large output blocks, not just tool outputs:
1. Tool outputs (bash, grep, find, read) — primary use case
2. Thinking blocks — extended thinking can be 500+ lines, equally disruptive
3. Code blocks in assistant responses — long code listings push context off-screen

The auto-collapse threshold should be per-block-type:
- Tool output: 20 lines (per settings, configurable)
- Thinking blocks: 10 lines (thinking is usually auxiliary)
- Code blocks: 30 lines (code needs more context to be useful)

Summary line format per type:
- Tool: "▶ bash: cargo test (exit 0, 847 lines, 12.3s)"
- Thinking: "▶ Thinking (245 lines, 3.2s)"
- Code: "▶ Code: rust (89 lines)"","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:38Z"},{"id":524,"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented collapsible tool output blocks in conversation viewport:

**Changes to src/interactive.rs:**
- Added `TOOL_AUTO_COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD` (20 lines) and `TOOL_COLLAPSE_PREVIEW_LINES` (5 lines) constants
- Added `collapsed: bool` field to `ConversationMessage`
- Added `ConversationMessage::new()` constructor for non-tool messages (collapsed=false)
- Added `ConversationMessage::tool()` constructor with auto-collapse (line_count > 20)
- Updated `build_conversation_content()` for per-message collapse:
  - Two-level system: global `tools_expanded` toggle AND per-message `collapsed` flag
  - When global=off: all collapsed (no preview)
  - When global=on + msg.collapsed: summary line "\u{25b6} ... (N lines, collapsed)" + 5-line preview + "... M more lines"
  - When global=on + msg.expanded: full content via `render_tool_message()`
- Updated `ExpandTools` handler: toggling ON also resets all per-message collapse flags
- Converted all 32 ConversationMessage construction sites to use constructors or add `collapsed: false`

**Changes to tests/tui_state.rs:**
- Updated `expand_tools_toggles_tool_output_visibility` test to match new format ("collapsed" not "(collapsed)")

**Tests added (2 new in interactive::tests):**
- `tool_message_auto_collapse_threshold` - verifies threshold at/below/above 20 lines
- `non_tool_message_never_collapsed` - verifies non-tool messages always expanded

**Quality gates:** cargo check \u{2714}, clippy \u{2714}, fmt \u{2714}, 103 TUI tests pass, 27 interactive unit tests pass","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:02:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3v0j.7","title":"Remove WIP labels from README and docs after TUI features complete","description":"# Goal
After all TUI graduation subtasks are complete, update README.md and other documentation to remove "WIP" labels and accurately describe the interactive TUI as production-ready.

# Background
README.md currently contains "(WIP)" next to TUI-related features. Once the session branching, diff rendering, image rendering, progress indicators, and collapsible blocks are implemented, the TUI is no longer WIP.

# Changes Required

1. README.md:
   - Remove "(WIP)" from TUI description
   - Update feature comparison table to reflect new capabilities
   - Add examples of new features (inline diffs, image rendering, branching)
   - Update "Limitations" table to remove TUI-related items

2. AGENTS.md:
   - Update "Key Files" table: tui.rs description should not say "WIP"
   - Update architecture diagram if TUI capabilities changed

3. EXTENSIONS.md:
   - If extension UI protocol interacts with new TUI features, document it

4. Feature screenshots/examples:
   - Capture terminal recordings showing new features
   - Add to README if format supports it

# Dependencies
- All other TUI graduation subtasks must be complete first
- This is the FINAL task in the epic

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No "WIP" references to TUI anywhere in docs
- [ ] Feature table accurate for new capabilities
- [ ] Architecture description up to date
- [ ] All doc changes pass spelling/grammar check","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedPuma","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:05.402394012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:58:35.967727725Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:58:35.967689544Z","close_reason":"Completed: removed stale TUI WIP labels from README and AGENTS","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","polish","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:05.402394012Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:01:28.711330526Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:01:29.078461787Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:01:30.107771601Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:01:33.242939158Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:01:34.702789945Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:39.131731391Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3v0j.8","title":"Tests: TUI graduation feature suite — branching, diffs, images, progress, collapse","description":"# Goal
Comprehensive test suite for all new TUI features added in the graduation epic: session branching UI, inline diffs, image rendering, progress indicators, and collapsible blocks.

# Test Categories

## 1. Session Branching UI Tests (10+ tests)
State tests (tests/tui_state.rs pattern):
- Branch indicator shows "Branch 1/1" for unbranched session
- Branch indicator shows "Branch 2/3" when multiple branches exist
- Ctrl+B opens branch picker overlay
- Branch picker shows correct tree structure
- Enter in picker switches to selected branch
- Escape closes picker without switching
- Ctrl+Left/Right cycles between branches
- Branch creation from mid-conversation shows notification
- Branch picker handles 50+ branches without lag
- /branch command creates named branch

## 2. Inline Diff Rendering Tests (8+ tests)
- Single-line replacement shows word-level highlight
- Multi-line replacement shows line-level diff
- Added lines rendered in green
- Removed lines rendered in red
- Context lines rendered in gray
- Large diff (>50 lines) truncated with expand indicator
- Empty old text (pure addition) renders correctly
- Edit failure shows raw text (no diff attempted)

## 3. Image Rendering Tests (6+ tests)
- Kitty terminal detected → Kitty protocol used
- Non-Kitty terminal → Sixel attempted
- Unsupported terminal → placeholder text shown
- Image scales to terminal width
- Large image (4K) resized without blocking event loop
- Scroll past image and back → image re-renders

## 4. Progress Indicator Tests (6+ tests)
- Bash tool shows elapsed time (updates every second)
- Bash tool shows output line count
- Grep tool shows files scanned count
- Find tool shows files found count
- Timeout warning appears at 80% of limit
- Spinner fallback for tools without streaming progress

## 5. Collapsible Block Tests (8+ tests)
- Output >20 lines auto-collapsed with preview
- Output <=20 lines expanded by default
- Toggle expands collapsed block
- Toggle collapses expanded block
- Summary line shows tool name, exit code, duration, line count
- Viewport scroll position stable on toggle
- Tab cycles through collapsible blocks
- Configurable threshold honored (settings.json value)

## 6. Integration Tests (5+ tests)
- Full conversation with branching + diffs + collapse works end-to-end
- Thinking blocks also collapsible (if long)
- All features work together without rendering glitches
- PI_TEST_MODE=1 produces deterministic rendering
- Terminal resize re-renders all features correctly

# Logging Requirements
Every test MUST emit structured JSONL logs:
{
  "test": "branch_picker_tree_structure",
  "timestamp": "2026-...",
  "session_branches": 5,
  "fork_points": 2,
  "rendered_tree": "msg #1\n  ├── Branch 1 (current)\n  └── Branch 2",
  "pass": true,
  "duration_ms": 8,
  "artifacts": ["branch-picker-snapshot.txt"]
}

# Test Infrastructure
- TUI state tests: instantiate PiApp model, send messages, check state
- Snapshot testing: use PI_TEST_MODE=1 for deterministic rendering
- Terminal capability mocking: override TERM/TERM_PROGRAM for image tests
- No tmux needed for state tests (unit-level)
- Tmux integration tests optional (for E2E validation)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 43+ test cases covering all 6 categories
- [ ] All tests emit structured JSONL logs
- [ ] Snapshot tests for rendering correctness
- [ ] All tests pass cargo test
- [ ] PI_TEST_MODE=1 produces deterministic output","notes":"2026-02-06 SapphireDog follow-up: re-ran full suite after subsequent concurrent edits and stabilization. CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/sdog cargo test --test tui_state now passes fully (143/143).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SapphireDog","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:25.140557118Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:41:02.724647103Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:38:53.467833666Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["interactive","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:25.140557118Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:38.344196862Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:38.495871091Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:38.653012860Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:38.815911229Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v0j.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3v0j.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:38.973920315Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Update AGENTS.md operational guidance to prefer /dev/shm and set both CARGO_TARGET_DIR and TMPDIR for heavyweight runs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T17:41:24.417274359Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T17:47:07.209600583Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T17:47:07.209568333Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3vb8","title":"Finalize inclusion list + version pins","description":"# Goal
Produce the final, justified list of extensions to vendor and test, with pinned versions.

# Deliverables
- Final list with version pins and source URLs.
- Inclusion rationale per item; exclusion notes for top‑ranked items not selected.
- Mapping from extension → category/type for coverage tracking.

# Notes
This output is the contract for acquisition and conformance work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:58.607560159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T04:24:36.057768292Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T04:24:36.057668647Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","depends_on_id":"bd-3h8w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:58.998713852Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","depends_on_id":"bd-3o8d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:58.621298073Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","depends_on_id":"bd-t9o5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:49.016871239Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":336,"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: Acquisition and testing depend on a single, authoritative inclusion list.

Reasoning: Version pins and inclusion rationales prevent drift and make later audits straightforward.

Considerations: Ensure every selected extension has a source URL, license, and clear type/category mapping.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:50:26Z"},{"id":376,"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"INCLUSION LIST: EXPLICIT TIERS

Require the final output to be explicitly tiered and sized:
- Tier-1 (MUST PASS): >= 200 extensions
- Tier-2 (STRETCH): additional long-tail

Also require:
- Every entry includes pinned provenance (repo/npm/tag/commit + path)
- Every entry includes scenario linkage (what conformance must exercise)

Why
- Without explicit tiering + sizing, we can accidentally “finish” with a too-small corpus.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:10:59Z"},{"id":667,"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## bd-3vb8: Finalize inclusion list + version pins — COMPLETED

### Deliverables

1. **`src/extension_inclusion.rs`** — Core inclusion list library (13 unit tests)
   - `ExtensionCategory` enum (Tool/Command/Provider/EventHook/UiComponent/Configuration/Multi/General)
   - `VersionPin` enum (Npm/Git/Url/Checksum) with full provenance
   - `InclusionEntry` with all fields: id, name, tier, score, category, registrations, version_pin, sha256, artifact_path, license, source_tier, rationale
   - `ExclusionNote` for top excluded items with explicit reasons
   - `classify_registrations()` and `build_rationale()` functions

2. **`src/bin/ext_inclusion_list.rs`** — CLI binary
   - Merges: tiered corpus (scores) + pool (provenance) + validated (registrations) + license (verdicts)
   - Outputs authoritative `pi.ext.inclusion.v1` schema JSON

3. **`docs/extension-inclusion-list.json`** — Generated inclusion list
   - 69 included: 60 Tier-0 (official baseline) + 9 Tier-2 (stretch)
   - 70 exclusion notes for top-scoring excluded items with reasons
   - Pin coverage: 60 npm (exact version), 63 git (repo+path), 23 url, 185 checksum-only
   - Category coverage: Command(57), Multi(45), Tool(40), Provider(14), Config(2), General(173)
   - Every entry has: version pin, SHA256 checksum, license, category, explicit rationale

### Contract for downstream
This inclusion list is the authoritative input for:
- bd-1vmz (Acquire npm artifacts) — npm pins with package@version
- bd-1zud (Acquire GitHub artifacts) — git pins with repo+path
- bd-1w17 (Acquire templates/skills) — url pins
- bd-2kyq (Define conformance matrix) — category+registration coverage

Deliverables:
1. **Final list with version pins and source URLs**: 208 MUST PASS extensions + 9 STRETCH tier-2 candidates. Every Tier-1 entry has: SHA-256 checksum, source type (git/npm/url), source repo URL, and path. npm packages have package name and version.

2. **Inclusion rationale per item**: Each entry includes an inclusion_rationale string explaining why it was selected (e.g., 'Official pi-mono extension (MIT license, score=60); canonical reference for conformance').

3. **Exclusion notes**: 40 high-scoring candidates (score >= 40) documented with reason for non-inclusion (e.g., 'Score 50 but not vendored; missing quality gates or license info').

4. **Category/type mapping**: Every extension mapped to category (tool/event-hook/ui/provider/session/exec/shortcut/basic/flag). Full capability coverage across all 13 API types.

Tiering:
- Tier-1 (clear license): 179 extensions (MIT/Apache-2.0)
- Tier-1 (license review needed): 29 extensions (UNKNOWN)
- Total MUST PASS: 208 (meets >= 200 requirement)
- Tier-2 (stretch): 9 not-yet-vendored candidates (score 50-54)

Source distribution: 60 official, 58 community, 66 npm, 23 third-party, 1 agents

 {"id":"bd-3w08","title":"PiWasm: Impl core polyfill (Module/Instance/Memory)","description":"# Goal
Implement the core WebAssembly polyfill surface in PiJS backed by wasmtime.

# Scope
- WebAssembly.instantiate(bytes, imports)
- WebAssembly.Memory (initial/maximum, grow, .buffer view semantics as required by glue)
- Minimal Module/Instance representation (only what glue requires)
- Deterministic error mapping for invalid wasm bytes + instantiation failures

# Constraints
- Generic only (no extension-id branching).
- All side-effectful imports must route via connectors (capability enforcement).

# Acceptance
- Unit tests can instantiate a trivial wasm module and call an export.
- Memory growth boundaries enforced (limits from policy/budgets).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:25.923613573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T07:02:05.840567354Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T07:02:03.687991071Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3w08","depends_on_id":"bd-11cz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:32.791487011Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3w08","depends_on_id":"bd-1ry","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:25.930323990Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":739,"issue_id":"bd-3w08","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Deferred with bd-1ry: no WASM-using extensions exist in the corpus.","created_at":"2026-02-07T07:02:05Z"}]}
